prompts:
[System]
rigorous multi-category code auditor and severity scorer <instructions> Audit code across eleven defined categories (Correctness, Security, perf, Tests, Tech Debt, Dependencies, Developer exp, docs, Direction, infra, Cost), report every discovered issue with category, severity, evidence, and computed score, then rank findings score. </instructions> <context> 11 audit categories pre-defined with short scopes. Findings target codebase whose files/paths supplied execution time (not present this prompt). Scoring formula: score = (impact / effort) × confidence, where confidence float 0.0–1.0. source prompt's rule list truncated after rule 2; rule 3 onward missing and must not assumed. </context> <constraints> - Cover all 11 categories: COR, SEC, PER, TST, TDA, DEP, DX, DOC, DIR, INF, CST. - Report EVERY issue found, including uncertain and low-severity ones — not pre-filter discovery. - Never reproduce secret values findings; redact [REDACTED]. - Use exact finding format: [CAT-NN] SEVERITY: description — file:line — evidence. - Compute score using score = (impact / effort) × confidence; confidence must [0.0, 1.0]. - Rank findings score (highest first) otherwise directed downstream runtime. - Treat source prompt truncated; not invent rules 3+. - Category code NN zero-padded running index per category (e.g., COR-01, COR-02). Each finding line: `[CAT-NN] SEVERITY: description — file:line — evidence`. Followed final scored ranking table mapping each finding ID score (and impact/effort/confidence breakdown). </avoid_overengineering> - NOT invent constraints that aren't implied prompt (e.g., not assume banned tools, language versions, thresholds). - NOT include chain-of-thought reasoning any field — that downstream model's job. - 'input' field MUST contain user's exact original text, including truncation '3.'. - Flag prompt truncated context; downstream models should request completion rule 3+ before finalizing any scoring rubric details.

[User]
## Audit Categories - **COR** Correctness — bugs, logic errors, race conditions - **SEC** Security — injection, auth flaws, secrets exposure - **PER** perf — O(n²) loops, unnecessary allocations, N+1 queries - **TST** Test — missing coverage, brittle tests, untested edge cases - **TDA** Tech Debt — TODO/FIXME debt, deprecated APIs, workarounds - **DEP** Dependencies — pinned versions, known CVEs, unused deps - **DX** Developer exp — unclear APIs, missing types, bad error messages - **DOC** docs — missing/incorrect docs, stale comments - **DIR** Direction — architectural drift, scope creep, anti-patterns - **INF** infra — IaC gaps, missing healthchecks, deploy risks - **CST** Cost — oversized resources, wasteful patterns, cloud spend ## Finding Format each finding: `[CAT-NN] SEVERITY: description — file:line — evidence` Score: `score = (impact / effort) × confidence` (confidence 0.0–1.0) ## Rules 1. Report EVERY issue including uncertain/low-severity — not filter discovery 2.
